{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Architect",
  "description": "Multixept Associates Limited an established architectural firm with over 25 years of experience. Founded in Lagos and now headquartered in Abuja, the firm provides architectural, project management, and construction-related services across Nigeria through its group of companies.? Multixept Associates is an architectural and design practice focused on delivering thoughtful, buildable and high-quality design solutions across residential, commercial and mixed-use developments. It operates within an integrated platform spanning architecture, construction and real estate development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Architect

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Architect is responsible for developing and coordinating architectural designs from concept through construction, ensuring that design intent is clear, buildable and aligned with project objectives.
	The role requires strong technical ability, attention to detail and close coordination with construction teams to ensure effective execution.


Key Responsibilities
Design Development:


	Develop architectural concepts and detailed design drawings blueprints, and 3D models.
	Translate project briefs into functional and buildable designs
	Prepare and review construction documentation


Technical Coordination:


	Coordinate with structural, MEP and other consultants.
	Ensure all drawings are complete, accurate and aligned.
	Resolve design issues during construction.


Construction Support:


	Work closely with Project Managers and Site Engineers.
	Respond to RFIs and site queries.
	Ensure design intent is maintained during execution.


Documentation &amp; Standards:


	Maintain drawing standards and documentation discipline.
	Ensure version control and drawing accuracy.
	Prepare schedules and specifications.


Success Metrics


	Accuracy and completeness of drawings.
	Minimal design-related site issues.
	Speed of response to site queries.
	Alignment between design and execution.


Requirements


	5&ndash;8 years of experience in architectural practice.
	Excellent use of Revit.
	Ability to produce detailed working drawings.
	Strong understanding of construction processes.
	Ability to read and coordinate engineering drawings.


Method of Applictaion&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter (PDF) to: admin@multixeptassociates.com using &ldquo;Architect&ldquo; as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-23",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Multixept Associates Ltd."
    
  },

Architect Job at Multixept Associates Limited




        
    

        
        Multixept Associates Limited an established architectural firm with over 25 years of experience. Founded in Lagos and now headquartered in Abuja, the firm provides architectural, project management, and construction-related services across Nigeria through its group of companies.? Multixept Associates is an architectural and design practice focused on delivering thoughtful, buildable and high-quality design solutions across residential, commercial and mixed-use developments. It operates within an integrated platform spanning architecture, construction and real estate development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Architect

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	The Architect is responsible for developing and coordinating architectural designs from concept through construction, ensuring that design intent is clear, buildable and aligned with project objectives.
	The role requires strong technical ability, attention to detail and close coordination with construction teams to ensure effective execution.


Key Responsibilities
Design Development:


	Develop architectural concepts and detailed design drawings blueprints, and 3D models.
	Translate project briefs into functional and buildable designs
	Prepare and review construction documentation


Technical Coordination:


	Coordinate with structural, MEP and other consultants.
	Ensure all drawings are complete, accurate and aligned.
	Resolve design issues during construction.


Construction Support:


	Work closely with Project Managers and Site Engineers.
	Respond to RFIs and site queries.
	Ensure design intent is maintained during execution.


Documentation & Standards:


	Maintain drawing standards and documentation discipline.
	Ensure version control and drawing accuracy.
	Prepare schedules and specifications.


Success Metrics


	Accuracy and completeness of drawings.
	Minimal design-related site issues.
	Speed of response to site queries.
	Alignment between design and execution.


Requirements


	5–8 years of experience in architectural practice.
	Excellent use of Revit.
	Ability to produce detailed working drawings.
	Strong understanding of construction processes.
	Ability to read and coordinate engineering drawings.


Method of Applictaion 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter (PDF) to: [email protected] using “Architect“ as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Content Assistant",
  "description": "HTS Africa is a fast-growing wholesale and retail fashion distribution company focused on making fashion products easily accessible across Africa. We specialize in streetwear and everyday fashion essentials while building a modern, systems-driven business powered by creativity, accountability, and continuous improvement.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Content Assistant

Job Location: Lagos

Description


	We are looking for ambitious, disciplined, and growth-minded individuals who are ready to contribute to a fast-paced startup environment and grow with the company long term.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with experience.


Benefits
At HTS Africa, you will have the opportunity to:


	Work directly within a growing fashion business
	Develop valuable real-world business experience
	Contribute to meaningful projects and operations
	Learn, grow, and take on increasing responsibility
	Be part of a young and driven team building for the future.


Note


	Please complete this application form carefully and honestly.
	Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for the next stage of the recruitment process.
	Estimated completion time: 10&ndash;15 minutes.

Content Assistant Job at HTS Africa




        
    

        
        HTS Africa is a fast-growing wholesale and retail fashion distribution company focused on making fashion products easily accessible across Africa. We specialize in streetwear and everyday fashion essentials while building a modern, systems-driven business powered by creativity, accountability, and continuous improvement.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Content Assistant

Job Location: Lagos

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for ambitious, disciplined, and growth-minded individuals who are ready to contribute to a fast-paced startup environment and grow with the company long term.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with experience.


Benefits
At HTS Africa, you will have the opportunity to:


	Work directly within a growing fashion business
	Develop valuable real-world business experience
	Contribute to meaningful projects and operations
	Learn, grow, and take on increasing responsibility
	Be part of a young and driven team building for the future.


Note


	Please complete this application form carefully and honestly.
	Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for the next stage of the recruitment process.
	Estimated completion time: 10–15 minutes.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Marketer",
  "description": "Lohachy Farms provides Nigerians with premium-quality farm-fresh eggs produced under the most hygienic and sustainable conditions. Our operations are guided by excellence, integrity, and a deep passion for agriculture.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketer&nbsp;&nbsp;

Job Location: Ibeju-Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a motivated and results-driven Marketer toactively seek out and engage customer prospects in their assigned territory.
	You will provide complete and appropriate solutions to boost revenue growth, customer acquisition levels, and profitability.


Key Responsibilities


	Business to Business (B2B) End-user Customer Acquisition, Management, and Retention (eg. Hotels, Bakeries, Confectionaries, Restaurants, Schools, Hospitals, Event Centers, Supermarkets, Corporate Organisations).
	Register and update client information in the company Registration Form.
	Generate sales reports each week and submit them to online platform.
	Coordinate sales effort with team members and other departments.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor&#39;s Degree.
	Proven work experience as a marketer.
	Familiarity with different sales techniques and pipeline management.
	Experience using mobile tools or CRM systems is an added advantage.
	Minimum of HND/Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field.


Essential Competencies:


	Excellent interpersonal communication skills.
	Customer Relationship Management.
	Digital Marketing Skills.
	Self-motivated and comfortable working with little to no direction.
	Presentation skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Marketer Job at Lohachy Farms




        
    

        
        Lohachy Farms provides Nigerians with premium-quality farm-fresh eggs produced under the most hygienic and sustainable conditions. Our operations are guided by excellence, integrity, and a deep passion for agriculture.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketer  

Job Location: Ibeju-Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a motivated and results-driven Marketer toactively seek out and engage customer prospects in their assigned territory.
	You will provide complete and appropriate solutions to boost revenue growth, customer acquisition levels, and profitability.


Key Responsibilities


	Business to Business (B2B) End-user Customer Acquisition, Management, and Retention (eg. Hotels, Bakeries, Confectionaries, Restaurants, Schools, Hospitals, Event Centers, Supermarkets, Corporate Organisations).
	Register and update client information in the company Registration Form.
	Generate sales reports each week and submit them to online platform.
	Coordinate sales effort with team members and other departments.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree.
	Proven work experience as a marketer.
	Familiarity with different sales techniques and pipeline management.
	Experience using mobile tools or CRM systems is an added advantage.
	Minimum of HND/Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field.


Essential Competencies:


	Excellent interpersonal communication skills.
	Customer Relationship Management.
	Digital Marketing Skills.
	Self-motivated and comfortable working with little to no direction.
	Presentation skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Human Resources",
  "description": "Nexia Nigeria - Our client, a leading agribusiness organization with operations across multiple states in Nigeria&mdash;is seeking to recruit an experienced and strategic Head of Human Resources to lead and strengthen its people and organizational development function.

The organization operates across crop production, livestock and animal management, irrigation systems, agro-processing, logistics, and sustainable agricultural services. As the organization continues to expand its operations and workforce, it seeks a dynamic HR leader who can build strong people systems, drive organizational culture, and support sustainable growth across all operational locations.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Human Resources

Job Location: Abuja (with periodic travel to programme states)
Employment Type: Full-time

Position Summary


	The Head of Human Resources will provide strategic and operational leadership for the organization&rsquo;s human resources function across all states of operation.
	The role holder will oversee recruitment, employee relations, performance management, organizational development, policy implementation, learning and development, and HR compliance.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead recruitment across all departments and states, including field-based hiring.
	Design and manage onboarding processes that properly ground new staff in the organization&rsquo;s mission and values.
	Lead HR policy development, review, and implementation.
	Coordinate staff capacity assessment processes in collaboration with department heads.
	Oversee learning and development tracking systems and staff development initiatives.
	Manage performance review processes and ensure alignment with staff growth and organizational objectives.
	Handle employee relations matters, grievance management, disciplinary processes, and workplace investigations.
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian labour laws and employment regulations across all operational states.
	Maintain accurate staff records, contracts, HR documentation, and payroll coordination.
	Support organizational development initiatives as the organization continues to grow.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Education: Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Business Psychology, Industrial Relations, Management, or a related field.
	Experience Baseline: 5&ndash;8 years of progressive HR experience, including at least 2&ndash;3 years in a senior HR role.
	Professional Certification: CIPM membership or equivalent professional certification is required.
	Multi-site Management: Experience managing HR across multiple sites or states within Nigeria.
	Organizational Development: Proven experience supporting organizational development initiatives beyond transactional HR.


Industry Advantages:


	Experience within agriculture, agribusiness, manufacturing, logistics, or field-based organizations is an added advantage.
	Experience working on donor-funded agricultural programs and projects is desirable.


Core Competencies:


	Strong understanding of Nigerian labour law and HR compliance.
	Excellent leadership, communication, stakeholder engagement, and interpersonal skills.
	Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving capabilities.
	High level of integrity, professionalism, and confidentiality.
	Ability to work effectively in fast-paced and geographically dispersed operational environments.


What We Offer


	Competitive remuneration package
	Health insurance coverage
	Career growth and leadership development opportunities
	Dynamic and collaborative work environment.

Head of Human Resources Job at Nexia Nigeria




        
    

        
        Nexia Nigeria - Our client, a leading agribusiness organization with operations across multiple states in Nigeria—is seeking to recruit an experienced and strategic Head of Human Resources to lead and strengthen its people and organizational development function.

The organization operates across crop production, livestock and animal management, irrigation systems, agro-processing, logistics, and sustainable agricultural services. As the organization continues to expand its operations and workforce, it seeks a dynamic HR leader who can build strong people systems, drive organizational culture, and support sustainable growth across all operational locations.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Human Resources

Job Location: Abuja (with periodic travel to programme states)
Employment Type: Full-time

Position Summary


	The Head of Human Resources will provide strategic and operational leadership for the organization’s human resources function across all states of operation.
	The role holder will oversee recruitment, employee relations, performance management, organizational development, policy implementation, learning and development, and HR compliance.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead recruitment across all departments and states, including field-based hiring.
	Design and manage onboarding processes that properly ground new staff in the organization’s mission and values.
	Lead HR policy development, review, and implementation.
	Coordinate staff capacity assessment processes in collaboration with department heads.
	Oversee learning and development tracking systems and staff development initiatives.
	Manage performance review processes and ensure alignment with staff growth and organizational objectives.
	Handle employee relations matters, grievance management, disciplinary processes, and workplace investigations.
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian labour laws and employment regulations across all operational states.
	Maintain accurate staff records, contracts, HR documentation, and payroll coordination.
	Support organizational development initiatives as the organization continues to grow.


Qualifications & Experience


	Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Business Psychology, Industrial Relations, Management, or a related field.
	Experience Baseline: 5–8 years of progressive HR experience, including at least 2–3 years in a senior HR role.
	Professional Certification: CIPM membership or equivalent professional certification is required.
	Multi-site Management: Experience managing HR across multiple sites or states within Nigeria.
	Organizational Development: Proven experience supporting organizational development initiatives beyond transactional HR.


Industry Advantages:


	Experience within agriculture, agribusiness, manufacturing, logistics, or field-based organizations is an added advantage.
	Experience working on donor-funded agricultural programs and projects is desirable.


Core Competencies:


	Strong understanding of Nigerian labour law and HR compliance.
	Excellent leadership, communication, stakeholder engagement, and interpersonal skills.
	Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving capabilities.
	High level of integrity, professionalism, and confidentiality.
	Ability to work effectively in fast-paced and geographically dispersed operational environments.


What We Offer


	Competitive remuneration package
	Health insurance coverage
	Career growth and leadership development opportunities
	Dynamic and collaborative work environment.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Human Resources & Corporate Services Manager Job at KannyVic Enterprise Consult




        
    

        
        KannyVic Enterprise Consult, a fast-growing agribusiness and climate-smart infrastructure company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources & Corporate Services Manager 

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	We are seeking an experienced HR & Corporate Services Manager to take full ownership of its human resource systems, organizational structure, and internal operations.


Qualifications
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor`s Degree with 7 years of experience.


We are looking for a professional who is:


	Highly structured and disciplined
	Experienced in managing performance-driven teams
	Confident in enforcing policies and accountability
	Capable of building strong organizational systems
	Oversee HR and administrative operations professionally
